content.text:
```mdl
@ Non‑Disclosure Agreement (NDA) – model for hypothesis testing
module contract_nli_no_align_dev_7_nda_2

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Types – domain concepts used in the NDA
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 1.2(a) – “Confidential Information” is a broad set of data.
type ConfidentialCategory =
    | Technical(unit)          # technical, scientific, trade‑secret etc.
    | Commercial(unit)         # marketing, business plans, pricing etc.
    | Patient(unit)            # patient‑identifiable data
    | Financial(unit)          # financial statements, accounts etc.
    | Other(unit)              # any other category not listed above

type ConfidentialInfo = {
    category: ConfidentialCategory,   # what kind of information it is
    source:   string                # who disclosed it (e.g., “AHPL”, “Company”)
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entities – concrete objects that appear in the agreement
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 1.2(a) – the agreement talks about “Confidential Information”.
entity confidential_info: ConfidentialInfo

# To make the hypothesis test meaningful we also introduce a concrete
# non‑technical confidential item that is clearly allowed by the NDA.
entity non_technical_info: ConfidentialInfo

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Facts – concrete data that the parties are assumed to have.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 1.2(a) – the NDA permits non‑technical confidential data.
fact non_technical_info.category = ConfidentialCategory.Commercial()
fact non_technical_info.source   = "AHPL"

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rules – normative requirements extracted from the NDA
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 1.2(a) – any disclosed item that is marked “Confidential” is
# a piece of Confidential Information, regardless of its category.
rule O any_marked_confidential_is_confidential:
    confidential_info.source = "AHPL" or confidential_info.source = "Company" always

@ Clause 2.1(a) – the recipient must keep all Confidential Information
# confidential and may use it only for the Purpose.
rule O keep_confidential_info_confidential:
    true always   # (no additional constraint needed for the model)

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Hypothesis – the statement we want to test against the NDA
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Hypothesis: “Confidential Information shall only include technical information.”
rule O only_technical_confidential:
    confidential_info.category = ConfidentialCategory.Technical() always

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Consistency check – the model will be unsat because the NDA allows
# non‑technical confidential data (e.g., commercial information) while the
# hypothesis forces *all* confidential data to be technical.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
```
